pages.text: Some animals must now be fed frequently to maintain their health. These animals include horses, mules, donkeys, dogs, wolves, cats, ocelots, camels and caribou. Most animals should be fed every 2-3 days by default. $(br2)Grazing animals, such as equines, camels, or caribou do not have to be hand-fed if kept on grass.

pages.title: Feeding
pages.text: Feeding an animal keeps it healthy. While not directly lethal, a starving animal may become immunocompromised and get sick easier, which may be deadly indirectly depending on the illness.
